svg.text: The dial → direction plane, split four ways: does the same forecast hold within each model
family AND each value axis on its own?
one dot per run · 56 modelable runs from experiments/spread_util_unified.json (round-1 record per run), split on the record’s
“organism” field (Qwen3-4B / OLMo-3-7B) and “axis” field (risk-seeking / insecure-code self-report)
Every panel shares the identical background = Δv_pred(4) = clip[−1,+1] of 4·ρ·σ (one selection step ρσ per round, wall-capped) — the
self-only force map (mixed-pool runs also feel the outside-source pull p−q); painted on the dots’ own endpoint-move color scale.
round-1 agreement ρ = corr(judge scores, value scores)
round-1 agreement ρ = corr(judge scores, value scores)
round-1 spread σ = within-prompt SD of value scores
round-1 spread σ = within-prompt SD of value scores
Dot fill = the run’s OBSERVED whole-run
endpoint move = clip[−1,+1] of 4 · ρ · σ
Pooled: 35 of 41. Both Qwen cells are
unanimous; every one of the 6 clashes sits
in OLMo’s risk cell. The OLMo self-report
cell is empty in the corpus.
Scope: 4-round runs only — the 11 eight-round judge-schedule runs are excluded and 7 runs are skipped for undefined ρ (zero within-pool spread), leaving 56 of
74 runs. R = 4 is every plotted run’s exact horizon. Mixed-pool runs also feel the outside-source pull (p − q), so the 4·ρσ background is the self-only force
map — sign and relative magnitude are what compare.
